reviews.review_msg: Hard to understand what he is saying. Bad pronunciation

DO NOT TAKE HIS COURSE. Bad English.  U can't read his hand writing. don't need to listen his lecture, if u took econ 103 or 105 before. he will only confuses u He does the pop quizzes at the very beginning of the class and if he likes you he will let you retake a quiz even if you are late. but if not, he won't even give you quiz paper to study.

He is nice, although he doesn't speak good English. He is always willing to help and he is not like some other professors who like to give students ridiculous amount of assignments. His exam is related to the textbook and assignments. He also posts practice exam, which is very helpful.
I like this teacher. Some teachers talk a lot in class, but you feel learn nothing. He is that kind of teacher who doesn't impress you at first, but soon you will find he is knowledgeable.
Tony is a nice dude and he's course is very straight forward and simple. Easy class and nice prof. what more do you want?
Nice prof. I just suck at economics.

Tony is a fair prof. His midterms are easy if you sit down, read the textbook and keep up with the material. To manage this class it's important to consistently keep up. Overall, Tony was a great prof. Make good use of his office hours as he is always willing to help!
HORRIBLE accent and just reads off powerpoint slides
good
He is clear about what he expects from his students. Lectures are easy to understand and helpful while studying. GREAT PROF OVERALL!  Read the book, do mel practice, and keep up with the assignments!
Hard to understand because of his accent. Classes are SUPER boring, don't bother showing. Do the online assignments and read the textbook.
Barely speaks english, super hard to understand, reading the textbook will probably be more useful than the lectures
He's a really nice Chinese  teacher.
He is a really nice prof, just attend the lecture and do the practice problem, you can get a good grade

Very nice guy, not the best speaker. He basically explains the textbook for you in lecture which I found quite helpful. when I try to study, my class notes match the textbook materials and it saved me a lot of time.  Go to his office hours ask him about how he would answer assignment questions and do exactly the same on the exam you will get an A.

VERY VERY BAD ENGLISH. His tests are so easy the class scaling may screw you over. Overall, I stopped attending lectures simply because he would just read his lecture notes out loud word for word. It's pretty annoying as well when he writes down an equation that's already written on the notes in front of you. Useless prof!
Good prof, midterms are fair, you can get up to B+ if you do all the textbook and online questions. Final was hard
he is nice,I learned a lot!
Prof Dr. Xiang is very clear and helpful

Yes, has a thick accent, but tries hard to make himself understandable. Nice guy. Helpful when approached. Fair exams. Lectures slides are exactly information in textbook. Easy A without attending lectures or tuts, just make sure you read the textbook on your own.

He's nice; fair exams. BUT HE CANNOT TEACH. At all. He is incoherent, unclear and has too much trouble communicating ideas to students...he's also really boring, monotone, and not engaging. everyone texts in class. The text/youtube teachers better imo. Be prepared to learn on your own.
he is very nice and helpful and always care about his students.i would like to take his course again!

Actually he is a very nice teacher. If you take notes in class and do every exercise, you can get a very good grade in final. He is very patient and answer questions. Although he is not as funny as other teachers, you can actually learn something from his class.
Incomprehensible and useless
have fun teaching yourself buddy.

He is a very nice professor.  His exams are very straight forward, though his notes are straight from the text book.  I didn't do good on the two midterms, but I ended up getting a decent grade.  Finals are relatively easy compare to other SFU econ prof.
Horrible Prof, Material is straight out of the textbook.  Too much material too, for the course! Horrible grade.

His lectures are boring, he doesn't give breaks in a 2hr lecture, and it also seems like he is reading the lecture slides to himself rather than projecting it to the class. Midterms were relatively easy compared to the final, where questions were asked on materials where he ironically said during lecture that we wouldn't need to know for the final.

More than half of the students didn't show up during lecture time. His lectures were basically the summary of the textbook. Midterms were easy but final was quite tough. So aced the midterms if u can =D

Although his teaching is quite boring, but I think he is one of the easiest economic prof. in SFU.   Exams are fair, just read his lecture slides and you are ready to go.  I didn't even use the text book at all  :)

I don't understand why SFU hires a guy with language prblem to teach a university course. He has accent and his speeches are broken. And even worse, he reads off power point slides 99% of time in the lecture. I've had enough that SFU hire a TA to teach economics courses. Where are the professors and where does our tuition go????
